template:
You are writing a system prompt for an LLM agent. A system prompt is the agent's permanent context — its role, its constraints, its operating principles. It is read once at the start of every conversation and shapes everything that follows.

Inputs:
- Agent name / role: {{agent_role}}
- Primary job (one sentence): {{primary_job}}
- Who the user is: {{user_profile}}
- Tools / capabilities the agent has access to: {{tools}}
- Hard constraints (must / must-not): {{constraints}}

Principles to honor while writing:
1. Lead with identity and job, not with rules. The agent should know *what it is* before it knows what to avoid.
2. Every rule should pay rent — name the failure mode it prevents. If you cannot name the failure, drop the rule.
3. Prefer positive instructions over negative ones. "Do X when Y" beats "don't do Z".
4. State the success signal: how does the agent know it has done its job?
5. Keep it scannable. The agent re-reads this every turn under pressure; long prose gets skimmed.

Output format:
- Section 1: Role and job (2–4 lines)
- Section 2: Operating principles (numbered, each a single sentence)
- Section 3: Tools and when to use them (if applicable)
- Section 4: Done criteria — when to stop, when to ask, when to hand back
- Section 5: A short example interaction (optional, only if it disambiguates)

Output in {{lang}}.

fieldValues.agent_role: [e.g. Code reviewer for a Vue 3 + Tailwind static site]
fieldValues.primary_job: [one sentence — what does this agent exist to do]
fieldValues.user_profile: [who is this agent talking to — junior dev, PM, end user, etc.]
fieldValues.tools: [Read, Edit, Bash, WebFetch, etc. — or N/A]
fieldValues.constraints: [non-negotiables — e.g. never modify package.json, always ask before deleting]

fields.name: agent_role
fields.default: [e.g. Code reviewer for a Vue 3 + Tailwind static site]

fields.name: primary_job
fields.default: [one sentence — what does this agent exist to do]

fields.name: user_profile
fields.default: [who is this agent talking to — junior dev, PM, end user, etc.]

fields.name: tools
fields.default: [Read, Edit, Bash, WebFetch, etc. — or N/A]

fields.name: constraints
fields.default: [non-negotiables — e.g. never modify package.json, always ask before deleting]
